title: Selaginella tenera
intro: Selaginella tenera, also known as Spiked Spikemoss, is a type of fern-like plant that belongs to the Selaginellaceae family. It has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Selaginella tenera include saponins, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Selaginella tenera is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of capsules, tablets, and tinctures.
taxonomy: Selaginella tenera is classified within the family Selaginellaceae, which is a group of vascular plants commonly known as spikemosses. This species belongs to the genus Selaginella, which comprises approximately 700 species of spikemosses. Selaginella tenera is further classified as a member of the order Selaginellales, which is a distinct group of plants that are thought to have originated over 300 million years ago. The order Selaginellales is part of the class Lycopodiopsida, which includes all the lycopods, a group of ancient vascular plants. As a member of the class Lycopodiopsida, Selaginella tenera shares characteristics with other lycopods, such as the presence of sporophylls and a distinct reproductive structure.
uses: Selaginella tenera is a species of plant that has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. Its leaves and stems are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful for reducing pain and swelling. In some cultures, Selaginella tenera is used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to reduce fever.
